overview: From 2026-06-05 to 2026-06-06, elizaos/eliza had 17 new PRs (18 merged), 2 new issues, and 9 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Steward login broken in prod: OAuth authorize 400 (code_challenge) + email/send 400 â€” blocks all Sign in with Eliza Cloud
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary
Steward login is broken in production â€” **both** email and Google OAuth fail with 400 â€” which blocks "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" for **every** app (the cli-login flow bounces all third-party apps + the dashboard through this same login).

Reproduces with plain `curl` (no browser, no extensions), so it's a server-side prod issue, not a client environment.

## Symptoms (prod, `api.elizacloud.ai`)
**Google/OAuth** â€” `GET /steward/auth/oauth/google/authorize?...&response_type=code`:
```
{"ok":false,"error":"code_challenge is required for response_type=code"}
```
Adding `code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method=S256` still returns **400**.

**Email** â€” `POST /steward/auth/email/send` â†’ **400 Bad Request** (from `steward-login-section.tsx` â†’ `signInWithEmail`).

## Root cause (the OAuth half)
The Steward `/authorize` endpoint now **requires PKCE** (`code_challenge`), but:
- `packages/cloud-frontend/src/pages/login/steward-oauth-url.ts` â†’ `buildStewardOAuthAuthorizeUrl()` builds the URL with `response_type: "code"` and **no** `code_challenge` / `code_challenge_method`.
- The exchange flow is **nonce-based with no verifier**: `packages/cloud-api/auth/steward-nonce-exchange/route.ts` documents *"The Steward API authenticates the exchange purely by possession of the one-time `code` â€” there is no client [verifier]."*

So the authorize service (PKCE-required) and the cloud login flow (nonce-based, no PKCE) are out of sync â€” and even a hand-added `code_challenge` still 400s, suggesting a deeper Steward auth misconfig/regression in prod.

## Impact
No one can sign in via Eliza Cloud right now â€” dashboard, edad, and every third-party app using the cli-login "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" bounce here and dead-end at this login.

## Fix options
- **A (restore parity):** make Steward `/authorize` accept the nonce flow **without** `code_challenge` (matches the existing nonce-exchange contract). Simplest if PKCE was enabled unintentionally.
- **B (full PKCE):** frontend generates a `code_verifier` + S256 `code_challenge`, sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and stores the verifier; `steward-nonce-exchange` forwards `code_verifier` to `/auth/oauth/exchange`.

The `email/send` 400 handler isn't in this monorepo (Steward service) â€” flag for that team too; it may share the same prod regression.

Found via the cli-login flow from a third-party browser app.

topIssues.title: plugin-local-inference: voiceâ†’entity binding (bindEntity) is never called at runtime â€” recognized speakers never persist to the relationship graph
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

In `plugin-local-inference`'s voice pipeline, the plumbing to tie a recognized voice to an elizaOS `Entity` is fully present, but the **bind step appears to be unreachable at runtime** â€” `VoiceProfileStore.bindEntity` / `unbindEntity` have no caller outside tests. As a result a profile's `entityId` stays `null` in practice: attribution can resolve *which voice* is speaking, but the agent never persists *who that is* into the relationship graph.

> Grep-level finding on current `develop` â€” happy to be corrected if there's a dynamic/registered path I missed.

## What already works (so this isn't a rebuild)

- Voice profiles carry and persist an `entityId` â€” `plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/profile-store.ts` (`bindEntity` L605, `unbindEntity` L627).
- Attribution resolves the entity on recognition â€” `src/services/voice/speaker/attribution-pipeline.ts:199` (`entityId: refined?.entityId ?? match.profile.entityId ?? null`).
- The UI already crowns the OWNER when the speaking voice's entity matches the owner â€” `packages/ui/.../ChatVoiceStatusBar` (`speakerEntityId === ownerEntityId`).

## The gap

`grep -rn bindEntity` across `plugins/` + `packages/` returns only the definitions in `profile-store.ts` and references in `__tests__/voice-profile-store.test.ts`. The voice-profile routes (`src/services/voice/voice-profile-routes.ts`) expose `activate` / list / soft-delete, but **no bind/claim endpoint**, and there is no agent action that binds. So there is no runtime path (route, action, or UI affordance) for "this voice = this entity."

Consequence: `entityId` stays `null`, the OWNER-crown only lights up in tests, and recognized speaker identity never reaches the relationship graph â€” the agent hears a known voice but can't remember *who* across sessions.

## Suggested shape (open to direction)

1. A bind/unbind route alongside the existing voice-profile routes, e.g. `POST /v1/voice/profiles/:id/bind { entityId }`.
2. An agent action so the agent can bind by voice ("this is <name>"), mirroring how `GENERATE_MEDIA` is wired.
3. On bind, route through `EntityStore` / `RelationshipStore` + the identity merge engine (per the root `CLAUDE.md` rule â€” don't bypass it), so the bound voice becomes a first-class relationship node.

Happy to take a first cut if this is wanted and not already in progress.

topIssues.title: App-credits: purchases fund the per-app pool but inference debits the org balance (stranded credits)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary
App-credits purchases and inference spend use **two different ledgers**, so credits a user buys for a monetized app can't be spent â€” and a user with cloud/org credits is blocked from a monetized app unless they separately top up the app pool. This blocks third-party browser apps monetizing via `X-App-Id` end-to-end.

This looks like a half-finished migration (see the `// Switched from app_credit_balances ... to the org balance` comment), so flagging rather than unilaterally changing billing.

## Details (origin/develop)
- **Purchase funds the per-app pool.** `processPurchase` credits `app_credit_balances` via `appCreditBalancesRepository.addCredits(...)` â€” `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/app-credits.ts:~307`.
- **Balance check reads the per-app pool.** `checkBalance` â†’ `appCreditBalancesRepository.findByAppAndUser(...)` â€” `app-credits.ts:~193`.
- **Spend debits the ORG balance.** The spend path was switched to `creditsService.reserveAndDeductCredits({ organizationId })` â€” `app-credits.ts:~408` (the "Switched from app_credit_balances ... to the org balance" comment). The `X-App-Id` completions path also reserves against the org: `reserveCredits({ organizationId })` â€” `packages/cloud-api/v1/chat/completions/route.ts:~934`.

## Impact
1. A user buys app-credits â†’ funds land in `app_credit_balances` â†’ inference debits the **org** balance â†’ "Insufficient credits" against an empty org balance. **Money paid, credits never spendable.**
2. `checkBalance` gates on the app pool, so a user who *does* have org/cloud credits is rejected from a monetized app unless they top up the app pool too.
3. The `/api/v1/apps/[id]/chat` 402 says "Insufficient app credits" but deducts from the org balance (`route.ts:~295-333`), so the error misdirects users.

## Suggested fix (pick one ledger)
- **A â€” org-only (matches the "Switched" comment's intent):** make `processPurchase` credit the **org** balance (and have `/api/v1/app-credits/balance` + `checkBalance` report/read org). App-credits checkout becomes "top up cloud credits for this app"; creator still earns markup via `recordCreatorEarnings()`. Simplest if the per-app pool is being retired.
- **B â€” app-pool-only:** make the `X-App-Id` spend path read+debit `app_credit_balances` instead of the org balance.

Happy to open the PR once you confirm the direction (A or B) â€” A looks consistent with the existing comment + `recordCreatorEarnings`. Also a one-line follow-up: word the `/apps/[id]/chat` 402 to match whichever ledger is checked.

Found while building a third-party app (SupaKan) on the `X-App-Id` app-credits flow.

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): add shared runtime provisioning path
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- add execution-tier aware provisioning so shared Eliza agents run through the shared runtime without container provisioning
- wire create/provision/lifecycle/bridge routing around shared vs container-backed execution tiers
- add the ComputeProvider seam, DigitalOcean provider implementation, billing exclusions for shared agents, and cloud E2E coverage for shared/custom/lifecycle/dashboard flows

## Behind-base impact
- `origin/develop` did overlap with our edits in `packages/cloud-api/v1/coding-containers/route.ts`, `packages/cloud-shared/src/db/repositories/agent-billing.ts`, `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts`, and `packages/ui/src/App.tsx`
- the full local branch also carried older UI/desktop commits that conflicted with current `develop`, so this PR is the focused cloud provisioning slice on top of `fd73d4b6f2`
- the only semantic conflict in the focused slice was `eliza-sandbox.ts`; it is resolved by preserving upstream `buildAgentInsertData` and threading `executionTier`/status through that helper

## Validation
- `bunx @biomejs/biome check --write <touched files>` passed; existing optional-chain suggestions remain in `packages/test/cloud-mocks/src/control-plane/server.ts`
- `bun test packages/cloud-api/__tests__/agent-bridge-runtime-routing.test.ts` passed, 2/2
- `git diff --check origin/develop..HEAD` passed
- conflict-marker scan over touched cloud paths passed

## Local validation caveat
- The original cloud branch with this focused code passed `bun run --cwd packages/test/cloud-e2e test` 18/18, `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend audit:cloud` 116/116, and `bun run verify` 460/460 before rebasing.
- In the rebased PR worktree, broader package typechecks/tests are blocked by local dependency hydration: `bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts` reports the lockfile would change, symlinked `node_modules` is missing required packages, and a detached verification checkout hit `No space left on device` before install. CI should re-run the full lane from a clean checkout.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): operator inspector and recovery controls
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This draft branch integrates the orchestrator/operator-inspector work into the elizaOS app and agent surfaces so it can be reviewed, tested, and selectively harvested. It adds richer orchestrator task APIs, operator recovery controls, edited-plan restart flow, timeline/message/usage plumbing, typed UI client coverage, and a workbench-style UI for inspecting tasks and sub-agent sessions.

This is intentionally a **draft**. The branch proves the end-to-end contracts and test coverage, but the UI should be reviewed critically before anything is treated as final visual/product direction.

## What changed

- Extended the orchestrator API surface for task detail, task lists, messages, events, timeline, usage, plan revisions, pause/resume, archive/reopen, fork, validate, retry, rerun, restart, and restart-with-edited-plan flows.
- Hardened orchestrator task service/store/mapper behavior around richer operator state, including sessions, artifacts, plan revisions, usage, active task/session counts, recovery state, and persisted task metadata.
- Expanded `OrchestratorWorkbench` with an operator inspector, task rail, sub-agent session cards, message/timeline rendering, plan/acceptance panels, recovery controls, plan editor, and message composer wiring.
- Added typed client methods and contract coverage for the new orchestrator API shapes in `packages/ui`.
- Stabilized app-shell route loading and smoke/live-stack behavior for `/`, `/chat`, `/orchestrator`, `/apps/tasks`, app routes, and settings/connectors paths.
- Added an env-gated local demo fixture mode for the smoke API stub: `ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1`. This lets reviewers open the normal app locally and see a populated orchestrator task with demo sub-agents without needing real provider credentials.
- Added detailed and simple HTML docs describing the branch, status, vision, validation, and how the orchestrator/operator inspector fits into elizaOS.

## Reviewer note for Shaw / agent review

The useful review path is probably:

1. Start with `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/api/orchestrator-routes.ts` and the matching service/store changes to verify the backend contract and recovery semantics.
2. Check `packages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts` and `client-types-cloud.ts` to confirm the frontend contract matches the route behavior.
3. Review `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` for product/UX direction, but treat this part as rough draft UI rather than final polish.
4. Review the smoke fixture in `packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-smoke-api-stub.mjs` as local test/demo support only. It is behind `ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1` and should not affect default smoke behavior.
5. Use the tests below to decide which parts are ready to keep versus split or refine.

## Local demo command

To see the populated local orchestrator surface without real agents/providers:

```bash
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_SKIP_BUILD=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_FORCE_STUB=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_API_PORT=31338 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_PORT=2144 \
node packages/app-core/scripts/run-node-tsx.mjs packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-live-stack.ts
```

Then open:

- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/orchestrator`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/apps/tasks`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/chat`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/`

The demo fixture seeds a task named `Build Kanban planner app` with `Codex Builder` and `Cerebras Reviewer` sessions, usage counters, timeline/messages, plan state, and operator controls.

## Validation performed

- `packages/app` lint, typecheck, and build passed.
- `packages/ui` typecheck and build passed.
- `packages/agent` lint, typecheck, and build passed.
- `plugin-agent-orchestrator` unit tests/build passed.
- `plugin-task-coordinator` unit tests/build passed.
- `plugin-app-manager` tests/build passed.
- App route/view/smoke coverage tests passed.
- Selector-based local route sweep passed for `/`, `/chat`, `/orchestrator`, `/apps/skills`, `/apps/trajectories`, `/apps/tasks`, and `/settings#connectors`.
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/orchestrator-gui-workbench.spec.ts` passed.
- Live demo stack was verified with Playwright: opened `/orchestrator`, selected the seeded task, confirmed the sub-agent inspector, sent a prompt through the composer, and confirmed the demo orchestrator response rendered in the message list.

Notes: app build still emits existing Vite dynamic-import warnings, and agent lint still emits existing warnings in `conversation-routes.ts`. Those were not introduced by this branch.

## Known caveats

- The UI is draft-quality and should not be merged as final visual design without product/design review.
- The local demo fixture is not a real agent runner; it is an env-gated stub for review and smoke testing.
- This is a broad branch. It may be worth splitting before merge into backend contract/service changes, UI workbench changes, smoke/test harness changes, and docs.
- No PR merge is requested from this branch as-is; this draft exists so reviewers can inspect the work and decide what to keep.

topPRs.title: Keep local first-run selection local

topPRs.body:
## Summary
- reset stale `cloud-inference` first-run state when the Local runtime card is selected
- add a regression test covering the Local card state reset
- suppress duplicate `Entity connected` debug log spam by logging only when room participants are newly created

## Verification

Focused Vitest was not run in this checkout because dependency linking is currently incomplete for nested `eliza` (`adze` is not linked from node_modules), and local Bun 1.3.9 cannot use the newer nested lockfile with `--frozen-lockfile`.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): let /goal autonomously drive the monetized-app loop (capped self-spend + economics capabilities)
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## Why

A `/goal` sub-agent is fenced to coding-only capabilities
(`DEFAULT_GOAL_CAPABILITIES`), and every paid Cloud command through the
parent-agent broker requires an explicit human "yes". Together these mean an
agent **cannot autonomously drive the build-monetized-app loop** (create app â†’
deploy â†’ monetize â†’ buy domain â†’ stay alive on earnings) â€” the loop the
`build-monetized-app` skill and the survival-economics cron were built for.

This PR adds two **opt-in** pieces so a goal task can drive that loop, with
**no change to any default behavior**.

## 1. Capped self-spend allowance (`spend-allowance.ts`)

Gate broker Cloud commands by a per-session USD budget
(`ELIZA_AGENT_SPEND_CAP_USD`, default `0` = today's confirm-everything behavior).

- `read` / `dry-run` â†’ never gated (unchanged).
- `destructive` â†’ ALWAYS require human confirmation, regardless of cap.
- self-spend commands (debit our own credits: `domains.buy`,
  `containers.create`, media/promote/ads) â†’ auto-authorize only while running
  total + estimated cost â‰¤ cap; over cap or unknown cost â†’ confirm.
- other mutating / revenue commands (`apps.create`, `apps.charges.create` â€”
  funded by the payer, not us) â†’ auto-authorize while the allowance is active.

Cost hints come from a reserved `spendEstimateUsd` param that is stripped before
the outbound request. The ledger is in-memory per child session (a safety
throttle â€” real money is still gated server-side by atomic debit/refund). The
pure decision logic is fully unit-tested.

## 2. Economics capability profile (`goal-prompt.ts`)

`ECONOMICS_GOAL_CAPABILITIES` extends the coding fence with the parent-agent
Cloud bridge (apps / containers / domains / charges / credits). A task opts in
via `metadata.capabilityProfile = "economics"`; `spawnAgentForTask` applies it.
The default coding-only fence is unchanged. UI: a leading `/economics` (or
`/monetize`) directive in the Odysseus composer creates the task with that
profile.

## Safety

- **Off by default**: cap `0` + the default profile reproduce today's behavior
  exactly.
- Destructive actions and over-cap spend still require a human.
- Auto-authorizations are logged for audit.

## Tests

New unit tests: `spend-allowance.test.ts`, `goal-prompt.test.ts`, broker
allowance integration cases in `parent-agent-broker.test.ts`, and
`composer-directives.test.ts`. All green; orchestrator typecheck clean. The
end-to-end money path these capabilities drive is covered by the smoke test in
#8249.

topContributors.summary: NubsCarson: Focused on stabilizing and enhancing the cloud infrastructure, successfully merging four PRs in elizaos/eliza that addressed critical OAuth authentication issues (#8259) and introduced new SDK sign-in capabilities (#8251). Their work was primarily driven by bugfix efforts, complemented by documentation updates and feature development to improve cloud-shared functionality and affiliate credit tracking.

2-A-M: Drove significant progress on the cloud infrastructure by implementing autonomous goal monetization and container deployment routes in elizaos/eliza (#8250, #8258), while ensuring system reliability through new smoke tests (#8249) and Windows compatibility fixes (#8248). Their work today was primarily focused on feature development and testing, accounting for 75% and 35% of their total code contributions respectively.

standujar: Focused on enhancing cloud infrastructure stability and functionality, merging three PRs in elizaos/eliza that secured Steward proxy requests via HMAC (#8256), resolved agent bridge_url connectivity issues (#8262), and exposed execution modes in the cloud frontend (#8261). Their work primarily centered on bugfixes and feature development, involving 1,398 additions across 13 files.

lalalune: Led a significant architectural overhaul of the elizaos/eliza codebase by merging two major PRs (#8247, #8246) that replaced the cloud infrastructure and cleaned up OpenRouter integrations, involving over 180,000 total line changes. Their efforts were primarily concentrated on bugfixes, which accounted for half of their 18 commits across 2919 modified files.

odilitime: Focused on refining local configuration workflows by opening PR #8263 in elizaos/eliza to ensure first-run selections remain local. This effort involved 6 commits across 10 files, with a primary emphasis on testing and general codebase maintenance.

0xSolace: Focused on enhancing cloud authentication security by implementing agent token minting with trade:order scope in elizaos/eliza (#8254). Their work today was evenly split between feature development and testing, involving 47 additions across 4 files.

LamboPoewert: Expanded the ecosystem's capabilities by implementing a new third-party registry plugin in elizaos/eliza (#8260). This contribution focused exclusively on configuration updates to integrate the @madeonsol/plugin-madeonsol package.
